skill.name: spark-optimization
skill.description: Optimize Apache Spark jobs with partitioning, caching, shuffle reduction, join tuning, and memory configuration guidance.
skill.summary: Practical guidance for improving Apache Spark performance and diagnosing slow distributed data jobs.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences, PySpark examples, diagnostic snippets, and documentation links. Review found no executable skill scripts, credential access, command execution, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior. Remaining risk is low because the skill contains external documentation links and operational Spark examples users may adapt.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations are Markdown diagrams, Python examples, Spark SQL examples, and inline API guidance. No evidence found that SKILL.md executes shell commands or invokes external processes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is inside Markdown documentation and describes Spark usage examples. There is no script file, command runner, or user input path that would create command execution risk.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Create Minimal Network Exposure
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for malicious network behavior. Lines 413-415 contain static links to Spark and Databricks documentation. They do not perform network requests or transmit user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are human-readable resource links in a Resources section. I found no evidence of automated outbound requests or exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: Credential and Reconnaissance Signals Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The certificate, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts come from words or APIs used in Spark diagnostics and examples. No evidence found of key file access, weak crypto implementation, or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Spark memory status, query plan modes, partition skew ordering, and best practices. They do not access secrets or inspect the local host environment.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The code execution, network, and credential access combination is not supported by semantic review. The file is documentation with examples and resource links only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous combination depends on separate false-positive signals. I found no executable mechanism that combines commands, outbound network traffic, and credential access.

content.value_statement: Slow Spark jobs waste cluster budget and delay analytics work. This skill gives practical tuning patterns for partitions, joins, memory, caching, and shuffle behavior.
actual_capabilities: Explains Spark execution concepts, including jobs, stages, tasks, partitions, and shuffles.
Provides partition sizing guidance and examples for repartitioning, coalescing, and partition pruning.
Shows join optimization patterns for broadcast joins, sort-merge joins, bucket joins, and skew handling.
Gives caching, persistence, checkpointing, and unpersisting guidance for repeated DataFrame use.
Documents memory, shuffle, compression, file format, and monitoring configuration examples.
limitations: It does not inspect live Spark UI metrics or cluster logs by itself.
Configuration values are templates and must be adjusted for workload size and cluster limits.
Examples use placeholder S3 paths and assume PySpark or Spark SQL familiarity.
It does not replace workload testing, benchmarking, or cloud cost analysis.

use_cases.title: Reduce Batch Pipeline Runtime
use_cases.description: Use the skill to review partitioning, shuffles, joins, caching, and file formats for a slow Spark batch job.

use_cases.title: Tune Databricks or EMR Jobs
use_cases.description: Apply the configuration cheat sheet to choose executor memory, shuffle partitions, broadcast thresholds, and compression settings.

use_cases.title: Debug Skewed Aggregations
use_cases.description: Use the skew detection and salting patterns to diagnose uneven partitions and reduce long-running tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Slow Spark Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the spark-optimization skill to review my slow Spark job. Focus on obvious partitioning, shuffle, caching, and join issues.

prompt_templates.title: Suggest Spark Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the spark-optimization skill to suggest Spark settings for a job that processes my data size, executor count, and join pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Data Skew
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the spark-optimization skill to diagnose possible data skew in this aggregation or join. Recommend checks and practical fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Optimization Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the spark-optimization skill to create a staged optimization plan for this Spark pipeline, including benchmarks and rollback checks.

output_examples.input: A Spark job spends most time in a large join and has uneven task durations.
output_examples.output: The response identifies likely skew, recommends checking partition counts, suggests broadcast or salting options, and lists validation metrics.

output_examples.input: A PySpark pipeline repeatedly reads and filters the same DataFrame before several aggregations.
output_examples.output: The response recommends selective caching, materialization, unpersisting after reuse, and avoiding cache on one-time transformations.

output_examples.input: A team wants default Spark settings for a medium batch workload.
output_examples.output: The response proposes starting values for AQE, executor memory, shuffle partitions, compression, and file partition size.

best_practices: Start with Spark UI evidence, then tune the largest shuffle, skew, spill, or scan bottleneck first.
Benchmark one change at a time with representative data and record runtime, spill, task count, and output size.
Use built-in Spark functions and columnar formats before adding UDFs or custom processing logic.
anti_patterns: Do not collect large datasets to the driver for inspection or downstream processing.
Do not cache every intermediate DataFrame without checking reuse and memory pressure.
Do not treat template executor settings as production defaults without workload testing.

faq.question: Can this skill optimize a Spark job automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance, tuning patterns, and configuration examples that users apply to their own jobs.

faq.question: Does it support PySpark and Spark SQL?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples cover PySpark DataFrame operations, Spark SQL configuration, and Delta Lake optimization concepts.

faq.question: Can it diagnose Spark UI screenshots or metrics?
faq.answer: It can help interpret provided metrics, but it does not connect to Spark UI or cluster monitoring systems.

faq.question: Is it useful for Databricks jobs?
faq.answer: Yes. Many patterns apply to Databricks, including AQE, Delta tables, Z-ordering, caching, and shuffle tuning.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide job code, data size, file format, cluster size, runtime symptoms, Spark UI observations, and recent configuration values.

faq.question: Does it include security-sensitive behavior?
faq.answer: No confirmed security-sensitive behavior was found. The skill is documentation with examples and external documentation links.
